## Interest on this VC
Scottish breakfasts (mainly served in tourist cafes and accomodation i.e. BandBs) always contain at least one type of &apos;pudding&apos; based on oatmeal, barley, beef fat and beef or sheep offal, traditionally served in a sheeps&apos; stomach. They are often associated with remote rural agricultural areas and regions. Haggis, especially, has cultural connections to territorial identity, being frequently eaten across Scotland (and beyond) within a &apos;Burns Supper&apos; to celebrate Scottish poet Robbie Burns. Black pudding from Stornoway (Western Isles LAU1) has PGI status. Such puddings need to be made on the Isle of Lewis to a specific recipe. Charles MacLeod (original producers of Stornoway Black pudding) has won multiple awards including six Great Taste Awards.

## Key local assets
Natural assets: relies on land use for livestock farming, also landscape/ scenery to link to tourism ventures (i.e. scottish breakfast puddings often served in tourist accomodation). Social assets: strong links throughout the VC including between farmer/crofter, butcher and touism provider. Cultural assets: Links to traditional knowledge for pudding production, but also crofting and livestock farming practices. Haggis and black pudding are also important cultural icons for Scottish food industry.

## Challenges
relies on tourism for a good market for selling produce (potential challenges from BREXIT/ COVID). Relies on good relations across the VC (i.e. farmers, pudding producers, tourism ventures, tourists). Relies on maintaining land use/ climate so it can be suitable for livestock farming - potential imapcts from climate change may alter this. Only Stornoway black pudding has PGI status, other pudding may be at risk from &apos;imposters&apos;. Black pudding is also made in Lancaster LAU1. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current and predicted. This VC is classified as Traditional.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is unspecified. traditional as have been created and sold in these areas for many years. The innovation is related to the whole VC. The land use systems involved are Grassland and Natural land. The local assets include Natural, social and cultural.

## Concluding remarks
Haggis and other breakfast puddings are made throughout Scotland, includuing in non-upland areas, however, Stornoway Black pudding is the only one with PGI status. In Badenoch and Strathspey, Speyside specialities produce puddings, and others are produced in Edinburgh or even England (Lancaster LAU1) within Scottish branding for teh supermarket chain, Aldi. Breakfast puddings are important for both within-Scotland market and as an export market.

## Dairy Cheese
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is FRANCE INRAE. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 FR. There are 20 dairies collecting milk of about 300 dairy farmers (mostly dairy-Ewe farmers, but also dairy-Goat farmers). Some of those farmers make their own chese two (&apos;mixte producers&apos;). DAiries and farmers interact at each dairy&apos;s level, but they have also interprofesionnels organization such as the PDO Brocciu. the 5 most important dairies, each one collecting 15 to 100 dairy producers, push farmers to produce more, even if they have to change to foreign breeds, or adopte more intensive feeding practices. The current production of dairy cheeses knows a disembedding process : it might be less intertwined with the local socioecological system, that is local population on one hand, natural resources on the other hand. A large part of dairies complement their local collect of milk with pruchase of foreign milk, coming from the French Continent or Sardinia.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Corsican moutains. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Corsica. Corsica is an island in the sea. It presents a great diversity of landscapes: plains, hillsides, deap and steep valleys Small ruminant farming has long been a transhumant activity, occupying all the region. For the past decades, farmers have settled, in the plains and hillsides. Some of them keep on using mountain pastures during Summer. They are scattered within the region, with no remarkable specialised sub-region. Dairies are mainly located in plains but they collect milk from farmers all around Corsica, some of whom process a part of their production as well.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 8722. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 0. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 2706. The total population (in thousand) is 0.334. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 38.6. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been 1.10%. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 38.6. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been 1.10%.

## Income and gross value added

## Tourism

## Employment
The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 3.60%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 16.20%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 80.20%.

## Interest on this VC
high reputation products, coexistence between mountainous area and plains, legitimacy to produce between dairies and on-farm cheese makers, competing access to milk, strategy of localisation and contribution to villages vividness, resources linked to pastoral breeding (breeds, feeding species) and their associated know-how are diminushing.

## Challenges
to (re)assemble resources that have been built and transmitted in the territory (breeds, feeding species.. ) and to be able to address the issues raisen by the agroecological transition. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current and Predicted. This VC is classified as Tradiotional and Innovative.

## Innovation
Dairies have been created in the 1980ies. These are mostly family businesses, they used to mature cheese or to make it from their own herd&apos;s milk and they decided to expand.Dairies produce a large range of cheeses, mostly embedding into local typicality. They are of different sizes and know standardization with various intensity.The most important ones are located in the Corsican plains, some of them are in the hill-sides. They collect farmers&apos;milk everywhere in the region but most of the volume is produced in the plains. The land use systems involved are Grassland, Natural land and agrosylvopastoral systems (schrubs, forests of oaks - accorns, meadows). The local assets include Natural and cultural.

## Concluding remarks
Dairy husbandry and production of Cheese is observable in every other MRLs (Italy, Serbia, Slovakia, Switzerland). Recognition of GIs and coexistence between artisanal and industrial production are also a common features of those areas. Dairies might not be located within the mountainous areas, but they can collect milk there and so have an influence on the matching SES.

Full narrative:
## Clairette de Die - Wine production targeted towards export
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is FRANCE CCVD. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 FR1. Clairette de Die is a AOC sparkling white wine in 32 municipalities of the Drome Valley. In 1971, the method of production of Clairette de Die wine was officially recognised as the &apos;ancestral process&apos;. Nowadays, Clairette de Die 71 644 hl of Clairette de Die are poduced each year by more than 300 operators and through large cooperative. Clairette de Die production is mainly large conventional monoculture and represents an increasing part of cultivated surfaces which present a challenge in term of biodiversity, water supply, land use and installation of new actors. However, almost 20% of Clairette de Die is organic production today, thanks to a policy of &apos;aid to conversion&apos; proposed by the AOP. Clairette de Die is currently facing a structural crisis: sales decrease and production are still increasing thank to public aid for converting in organic. The crisis has been accelarated by the COVID crisis which generated a great amount of unsaled stock, namely organic wine and grapes. In terms of marketing strategy, Clairette de Die is mainly driven by large-scale distribution and export. However, it is currently facing a loss of attractivness, accelerated as well by the COVID crisis. There is a need to develop renewed marketing strategies driven towards local demand and supply chains. However, there is tensions between different visions of Clairette de Die operators.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Drome Valley. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Drome departement. Drôme is a very diversified departement in terms of geological, natural and agricultural resources. The North-East of the territory is characterised by middle and high mountains (Pre Alps and Vercors Natural Park): agriculture is this area is mainly livestock and small diversified gardening. The South is characterized by the beginning of the Provence and the mediterranean climate, we can find there aromatic and yard large cultures. The West of the territory is characterized by plains were the Rhône and the Drôme rivers meet. Agriculture ther is mainly large cropland. Within the Drôme departement, Biovallée territority gathers 3 associations of municipalities (EPCI) - 95 muncipalities in total. It is a very preserved and diversified territory, following the Drôme River, from Pre Alps to large plains of the downvalley.In terms of organic farming, since the 1970s, this valley is a fertile land that claims to be &apos;the first organic territory in France&apos;. In 2017, there were about 35% of certified organic farmers. The goal is to reach 50% of farmers and surfaces in organic in 2020.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 2200. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 50. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 2453. The total population (in thousand) is 58.56. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 42.3. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been 1%. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is unspecified. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been 0.6%.

## Interest on this VC
Clairette de Die is a natural sparkling white wine, emblematic of the Drome Valley and recognised as an AOC (appelation d&apos;orgine controllée) and for its &apos;ancestral process&apos;. With more than 300 operators, 71 644 hl of Clairette de Die are poduced each year, mostly driven toward long supply circuits and export however 20% of the Clairette de Die AOC in organic. The Clairette de die filiere needs to develop innovative processes (promotion of organic production, adaptation to water scarcity and climate change), innovative marketing strategies (local channels vs. Long supply channels and export), innovative governance system (better cooperation with new actors).

Due to the climatic crisis, the VC depends on regional public aids. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current. This VC is classified as Traditional.

## Organic Iberian goose
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is SPAIN UCO. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 29 ES. Ganso Ibérico (Iberian Goose) is the only company producing goose in the dehesa. The farm has a field of 1500 ha of dehesa where the geese live freely in plots of 200 ha that they rotate to maintain the grass. Their product has the organic certification. They import the goose chicks from France when they have less than 2 days of age. The breeds they use are Toulouse and Embdem. They work with batches of 2000 geese and their total production goes beyond the 9000 animals per year. They sell eggs, raw meat and some elaborated products mostly to restaurants and gourmet stores. Most of their production is exported outside Spain (around the 80% in 2013).

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Sierra Morena. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Constantina. The urban settlement of Constantina is declared Good of Cultural Interest for its historical heritage and architecture. The temperatures are mild with high rates of precipitations for the region. The landscape is dominated by mediterranean forest and dehesa landscapes (holm oaks and cork oaks mostly).

Full narrative:
## Production of traditional edible products from the mountainous area of Gergeri, Fortyna (Psiloritis mountain)
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is GREECE. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 GR. The cooperative activities have an important impact on the regional SES, as they offer an opportunity for work and entrepreneurship to women. Their products are under quality schemes that meed the market standards and used in local host and touristic activities. The trademark of the cooperative is recognizable in Crete.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Psiloritis Mountain. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is GERGERI. The area is on the east hillside side of Psiloritis (Ida) mountain. Psiloritis is the highest mountain in Crete and among the highest in Greece, characterized as a Unesco Global Geopark. Phrygana and macchia vegetation dominate up to an altitude of 1,600 meters. The typical maquis vegetation of Psiloritis includes prinos, while scattered are piles of rough pine, cypresses and maples that form clusters up to a height of 1,800 meters. At higher altitudes the vegetation consists of bushes mainly of thorny plants, such as heather. The community of Gergeri was connected with the forest of Rouvas, which is the largest source of kermes in Europe. With water sources and the unique creation of the Cretan land.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 465.2. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 0. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 1572. The total population (in thousand) is 315.162. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 119.40. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been 0.02%. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 76.5. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been 0.01%.

## Income and gross value added
The average per capita income (EUR/year) [Province] is 13800. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Province] is 3752.14. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 5.39%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 11.64%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 82.97%. ## Employment

## Interest on this VC
In the Cretan delicatessen, a series of traditional edible products can be distinguished due to their taste and the nutritional value. A local women cooperative in the area of Gergeri produces successfully such products for more than 20 yeears. The products are strongly connected with the area due to the land morphology and the high quality of the local raw materials. Gergeri is located 40 kilometers southwest of Heraklion in the eastern slopes of Psiloritis mountain. Besides the innovative products, the women&apos;s cooperative of Gergeri produces a wide variety of crackers, cookies, breadsticks as well as traditional Cretan pies and vegetable pies. The cooperative has modern facilities for the preparation, production and standardization of products and maintains a beautiful country tavern for hosting social events. Their products are sold through online stores. The set of activities of the cooperative contribute to the local SES.

## Key local assets
The raw materials and products used by the cooperative are provided mainly by the local natural resources, the grassland and natural land. The women&apos;s cooperative has an important impact for the area of Gergeri, as well as for every mountainous area in Crete, because it is a success story for the female and montainous entrepreneurship, adapted to the environment and the tradition. Most of their products have a reference to the Cretan cuisine and heritage.

## Challenges
The women of the Cretan highlands have limited and difficult access to occupations other than farming activities in family level, which makes them completely dependent and deprives them of the prospect of personal and occupational choices. The majority of young women of the mountainous areas look for ways to leave their home villages, exacerbating the decline of the local population. Gergeri&apos;s women&apos;s cooperative is a successful example for tackling this social problem. The products they produce are of high quality, in close relation to the local raw materials and of high reputation in the Cretan market. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Endogenous and Exogenous. The women of the cooperative use local raw materials and products, processed with innovative methods that follow the traditional recipies and standardize the final product for the market. The coopeative has introduced a few innovative products (e.g. glass vases with pickled wild artichoke hearts in olive oil), which are distributed through supermarkets, delicatessen shops as well as several e-shops. The innovation is related to New products, New processes, New marketing strategies and New governance systems. The land use systems involved are Grassland and Natural land. The local assets include Natural, social and cultural.

## Concluding remarks
Women&apos;s cooperatives and local products can be found in many other MRLs.

## Island Wine from Lipari, Aeolian Islands
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is ITALY VINIDEA. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 29 IT. The viticultural activity on the island is performed by a small number of individual wine producers mostly concentrated on the production of sweet wine. As this type of wine is not easy to commercialise, an innovative wine producer decides to amplify his production introducing high quality dry wines. At the same time he implements a new marketing strategy leaning on the huge touristic flow in the area offering a possibility to stay in a modern wine resort and numerous tailor-made tasting and gastronomic experiences. The wine is distributed in the folowing main channels: private clients (tourists), e-commerce and export.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Aeolian Islands. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Lipari. Lipari is the largest of a chain of the Aeolian islands in a volcanic archipelago situated in between Vesuvius and Etna in the Tyrrhenian Sea off the northern coast of Sicily, southern Italy. As a result of its volcanic origin, the island is covered with pumice and obsidian. From April till October the area receives an important touristic flow.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 89.72. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 0. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 602. The total population (in thousand) is 12.5. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 139.04. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been 1070%. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 189. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been -3.30%.

## Income and gross value added
The average per capita income (EUR/year) [Province] is 9915. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Province] is 10300.8. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 2.40%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 12.50%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 85.10%. The per capita income (EUR/year) [Region] is 9119. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Region] is 80077.2. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 4.10%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 12.50%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 83.40%.

## Tourism
The total number of bed places (BPs) in tourist accommodations/year in the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 6203. The regional (Region) share of total BPs/year is 4.10%. The number of agricultural holdings in the LAU is 6129. The nearest settlement with population over 100000 (km) is 84. In this region protected areas are present.

## Employment
The primary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 5.70%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 13.90%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 80.40%. The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 7.80%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 13.70%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 78.50%.

## Interest on this VC
High quality wines, value creation from the recuperation of traditional varieties (Corinto Nero) and viticultural techniques (alberello pruning system), active engagement of tourists as main consumers and ambassadors of the product in Italy and abroad, successful implementation of e-commerce and active use of export channel. Traditional wine produced in the island under the denomination &apos;Malvasia delle Lipari&apos; is a sweet dessert wine from the dried Malvasia grapes The main actor of the VC introduced a range of high quality dry wines using Corinto Nero variety with was on the verge of the extinction. He also implemented new marketing strategies adressed to massive touristic flow on the island.

## Key local assets
Key local assets involve mainly specific lanscape and scenary, sea resorts and unique geological formations as Kaolin Quarries which are attractive for tourists from Italy and abroad. The main resources for the local viticulture are the autochnotous grape varieties (Malvasia and Corinto Nero) and volcanic soils, having specific characteristics valuable for wine production.

## Challenges
A common challenge for small island viticulture is the isolation from the main land which causes extremely high cost of production. Steep slopes and traditional type of pruning makes mechanization impractical and requires a lot of manual operations. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Predicted. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Endogenous and Exogenous. First innovation of this VC is the return to using of an abandoned autochtonous variety from the area, recuperated thanks to mass and clonal selection. Second innovation is the implementation of a high-level wine resort in the middle of the vineyards and offering a wide range of tasting and gastronomic activities. The innovation is related to New products and New marketing strategies. The land use systems involved are Cropland, natural land and Vineyard and other cultures in the area as olive trees and cappers. The local assets include Natural.

## Pumpkin seed products in Boldva
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is HUNGARY. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 HU. Within a social employment programme, initiated by the local government, members of the local community (poor, unemployed, Roma) produce pumpkin seeds and process it into different products in a small, local processing plant, on environmentally friendly ways. The programme provides employment for disadvantaged people, uses cropland on a traditional, but environmentally friendly way and provides healthy food products to urban customers through short food chains.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Northern Medium Mountains. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Boldva. Boldva village in Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén county, Edelényi district. It is a settlement located in the northwestern part of the county, the closest bigger city Miskolc is 15 km away and Budapest is 196 km away.

## Geography and population

## Interest on this VC
In addition to other vegetables and herbs, peeled seedless pumpkins began to be grown in Boldva, in the framework in a public employment based social enterprise, giving livelihood to poor, disadvantaged social groups (mainly Roma). A small processing plant, including high tech solar drying equipment was built, branding, design, a small shop was developed and short food chains approached with the products in the area. The municipality initiated this project, which is not common, there is a high level of co-operation and community spirit in the village in the framework of a social enterprise. The products are traditional, however, well designed and packaged for wealthy customers, sold through short chains. They imply solar energy during production and processing and use social networks and ICT for marketing.

## Key local assets
The VC uses the natural resources of the area, they cultivate a pumpkin plantation to produce raw material for the pumpkin seed oil and also produce herbs. The cooperation within the village, the support of the local authority, the local human resources are cornerstones of the project. They have become part of the local territorial quality assurance system.

## Challenges
Challenges in connection with climate change (draughts, extreme weather events, etc.) and the potentially less yield due to these issues. Difficulties of working with disadvantaged social groups and continouosly changing policy environment. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current and Predicted. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Endogenous and Exogenous. The municipality s program provides jobs for locals and produces high-quality products that are sold through both short and long supply chains. They use solar energy for drying. The design of the products is beautiful and demanding. The project is the mayor&apos;s initiative and is implemented with the locals - the innovation is endogenous. However, the financial support that enabled the process was coming from the government, through an employment programme. The innovation is related to New products, New processes, New marketing strategies and New governance systems. The land use systems involved are Cropland. The local assets include Natural, social and cultural.

## Concluding remarks
The initiative is part of the local territorial quality mark system.

## Cultivation and processing of organic apple on abandoned lands
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is ITALY UNIMOL and UNIPI. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 IT. &apos;Melise&apos; has involved the local administration, 50 local farmers and an extra-regional entrepreneur. Lands and clime &apos;collaborated&apos; to innovate local agricultural production (apples). Local processing industries are also involved in the value chain as tourist actors. A sort of territorial quality brand (Prodotto a Castel del Giudice) as been also designed to promote the socio-environmental context.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Central Appennines Molise. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Castel del Giudice. Castel del Giudice is a tiny mountain municipality (Province of Isernia) in Molise Region, Southern Italy. It is a town located in Italy&apos;s inner area, distant around 50 minutes from cities, that can offer essential services in the education, health and mobility sectors. This municipality is a member of the &apos;Borghi Autentici d&apos;Italia&apos; association that brings together small and medium-sized municipalities to promote a sustainable local development model to rediscover Italian villages as places to live, support and preserve.

## Interest on this VC

Here positive environmental (e.g., reduction of soil erosion), economic (like job opportunities) and social (e.i., chance to not emigrate) aspects are evident. Melise is also involved in other projects on honey production, &apos;agricultural&apos; beer and rural tourism. New products are marketed (apples, also indigenous varieties, as fruit, jams, fruit juices, etc.) and a new production process is implemented (organic farming), and the territory, as a natural and socio-cultural context, is enhanced as a quality brand.

The land use systems involved are Cropland. The local assets include Natural, social and cultural.

## Lamb production - COVAP (Cooperativa Ganadera del Valle de los Pedroches)
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is SPAIN UCO. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 30 ES. COVAP is one of the biggest Spanish cooperatives with 4500 livestock partners, located in Los Pedroches region, in Sierra Morena. It counts with 789 meat sheep farms of the breed merina. The cooperative is the key actor, which includes the producers, workers related to the feeding porducts, workers of the slaughter houses, lab technicians, inspectors, administrators and commercials. Simultaneosly, while the activity is totally dependent on the dehesa ecosystem, it could harm it if overgrazing occurs.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Sierra Morena. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Pozoblanco. It is the biggest and the most populated municipality of the northern area of Córdoba (NUTS3), and the capital of Los Pedroches region. The landscape is similar along the region, dominated by extense dehesas of holm oak and cork oak used for extensive livestock.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 329.86. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 280. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 726. The total population (in thousand) is unspecified. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 52.16. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been -3.30%. ## Income and gross value added
The average per capita income (EUR/year) [Province] is 15648. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Province] is 13153.1. The number of agricultural holdings in the LAU is unspecified. The nearest settlement with population over 100000 (km) is 70.6. In this region protected areas are present.

## Employment
The primary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 13.80%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 17.10%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 57.00%. The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is unspecified. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is unspecified. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is unspecified.

## Interest on this VC
High quality products with high reputation in national and international markets. COVAP is a cooperative of producers of animal products. It is connected to the social-ecological-system as the sheeps graze in the dehesas of the region. 1) New processes in terms of covering all the meat industry processes within their cooperative, including the laboratory anlysis; 2) New marketing strategies because of the export oriented approach and the possible certifications.

## Challenges
1) Keep growing in the international markets; 2) Keep energising the livestock industry in the region to maintain the production while avoiding overgrazing. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current and Predicted. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Endogenous. The innovation is that the cooperative coins every part of the value chain, from the animal production, feeding products, slaughter and transformation, and commercialisation. It works together with researchers to control and improve the quality. It is the one of the few slaughter houses and transformation centres in Spain certified to export meat to the United States. In addition, the installations of the slaughter house are certified to produce organic and halal meat. There is also an e-commerce platform in their website. The innovation is related to New processes and New marketing strategies. The land use systems involved are Grassland and Natural land. The local assets include Natural and social.

## Concluding remarks
The VC is present in other provinces of Sierra Morena such as Seville and Huelva. ## Interest on this VC

Jams and juices are produced from different types of NTFP in the region of Maleshevija (wild strawberrry; blue berry; red raspberry; black berry, plum&apos;s jams) contributing thus to rural families in that region, who are involved in such activities.

## Challenges
Harvesting is often done in a non-sustainable manner, careless about the resource, one due to the lack of training, and second due to the competition among harvesters to get quantity over quality. Buyout values quantity and rarely quality. Buyout is often done via informal (illegal) buyout channels, thus it involves several middlemen. The buyout prices are generally low. The stakeholders are disorganized, though some efforts of organization exist. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current and Predicted. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Volcanic Wine (Sicily-Etna)
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is ITALY VINIDEA. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 30 IT. Local producers were able to add value to their products, selling most of their wines to tourists at cellar gate, and consituting a basket of products of the territory that are sold together in winery and local shops.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is 0. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is CASTIGLIONE DI SICILIA. The municipality of Castiglione di Sicilia is the biggest of the Etna area, where are placed than 70% of the Etna wineries.

The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is unspecified. The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 11.03%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 16.99%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 71.98%.

## Interest on this VC
very successful development of mountain viticulture (400-800 m.s.l.), value creation on traditional grape varieties (nerello Mascarese), strong appellation identity (Vini Etna DOC), integration with turism of the area. success story of mountain viticulture, complementary with other agriculture value chains (olive oil, saffron, pistachio), and very well integrated with turism of the area.

## Key local assets
The 3500 altitude Etna volcan - sometimes in active eruption - attract great number of turists from all over the world. The local farmers were able to hook up on turism offering visitors a larger and more complete experience of discovery of the territory and its productions.

## Challenges
In the &apos;90ies several well established Sicilian wineries (Franchetti, Tasca d&apos;Almerita, Donna Fugata, Planeta etc.) bought vineyard lots, started producing and promoting the Etna wines on the domestic and international market were they were already present. The increased value and reputation of Etna wines fostered several vinegrowers to produce commercial wine, and farmers to plant vineyard. Nowadays, Etna vineyard is composed by 1100 hectars cultivated by 140 wineries, and became an essetial component of the Etna economy. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Endogenous and Exogenous. The main original innovation was the deepen understand of the agronomical traits of the main local variety Nerello Mascarese, and its adaptation to volcanic soils and mountain climate, thanks to the knowledge brought by the experienced wineries from outside the region. Later, the use of oak wood casks for Nerello fining, was optimzing the intrinsec quality of the red wines. In the last 15 years took place an increasing adoption of organic, biodynamic, natural, and quantic standards for production. An active consortium Vini Etna DOC and an institutional Enoteca Regionale, respectively for control of origin and typicity and for promotion of the appalletion wine, synergically support the marketing activities of the private enterprises. The innovation is related to New processes, New marketing strategies and New governance systems. The land use systems involved are Natural land and olive trees (local variety biancolillo), hazelnut, pistachio, saffron. The local assets include Natural.

Full narrative:
## Wines of Winningen, Mosel Valley
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is GERMANY VINIDEA. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 01 DE. The planted vineyard area is around 89 ha and is cultivated by 44 wineries - so the structures are quite small. Like the Moselle in general, Winningen is known for white wine (about 90% of the vineyard area) and especially for Riesling. Unlike other sections of the growing region, many more dry wines are produced here. This is thanks to the village&apos;s most famous winegrower Heymann-Löwenstein, who has been producing mainly dry wines since the early 1980s and was thus one of the pioneers on the Moselle. In 2005, one of his wines was named best foreign wine in Paris. He also worked to ensure that the &apos;Uhlen&apos; vineyard was the first one in Germany to receive three small appellations contrôlées within only one vineyard.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Rhenish Massif. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Winningen. Winningen is located on the Lower Moselle only about 10 km from Koblenz, where the Moselle flows into the Rhine. Many years ago, terraces were built by hand in order to cultivate vines on the extremely steep slopes. The terroir is characterised by Devonian slate of various formations and is mainly known as the home of powerful Riesling wines, which in the 19th century were as expensive as the top crus from Bordeaux and Burgundy.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 6.65. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 75. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 210. The total population (in thousand) is 2.421. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 364. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been -1.80%. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 185.61. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been 0.50%.

## Income and gross value added
The average per capita income (EUR/year) [Province] is 34600. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Province] is 6043.8. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 0.76%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 36.27%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 62.96%. The per capita income (EUR/year) [Region] is 34600. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Region] is 45321.94. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 0.88%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 31.73%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 67.38%.

## Tourism
The total number of bed places (BPs) in tourist accommodations/year in the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 254. The regional (Region) share of total BPs/year is unspecified. The number of agricultural holdings in the LAU is 706. The nearest settlement with population over 100000 (km) is 10.2. In this region protected areas are present.

## Employment

The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 71.95%. The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 1.40%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 26.28%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 72.33%.

## Interest on this VC
Wines from extremely steep, terraced vineyards (70% slope) with low yields and high quality. The vineyards are characterised by many small plots with their own microclimates and different slate formations (red, blue and grey slate) and are habitats for rare animals such as the Apollo butterfly. Due to climate change, the comparatively warm slopes are heating up. The careful work of the winegrowers enhances the reputation of the wines and ensures a clear and unique style. They are distinguished from the rest of the Moselle wines by their mix of opulence and delicacy.

## Key local assets
Winningen is located in a nature reserve. This winegrowing municipality has won several awards, making it one of the most beautiful villages in Germany. It is famous for the &apos;Moselfest&apos;, the oldest wine festival in Germany.

## Challenges
The vineyards cannot be mechanised. For many wines produced there, apart from a few well-known wineries, no adequate prices are achieved in relation to the production effort. Organic viticulture is therefore not or hardly possible. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is unspecified. For some years now, the municipality has been receiving more attention through the activities of the young generation of winemakers. In 2020, a young winemaker from Winningen was named Rising Star of the Year by Germany&apos;s most important wine guide. The innovation is related to New processes and New marketing strategies. The land use systems involved are Cropland and Vineyard area. The local assets include Natural and Cultural.

## Concluding remarks
Comparable terraced vineyards can be found in particular on the Moselle and its tributaries the Saar and Ruwer. The most famous example is certainly the &apos;Calmont&apos; in the village of Bremm, which is the steepest vineyard in Europe. In addition, terraces have also been created in other mountainous regions such as Baden, Rheingau and the Middle Rhine.

## Lamb meat from an autochthonous sheep breed
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is PORTUGAL ESTRELA. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 PT2. Serra da Estrela Lamb PDO meat comes from lambs of Bordaleira Serra da Estrela breed that are fed only with breast milk. At slaughter, they have a live weight of up to 12 kg and an age of up to 30 days. Carcasses weigh up to 7 kg. This product is sold locally and in large urban centers, especially during festive seasons. It is, also, cooked and served in local restaurants.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Cordilheira Central (RR08). The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Manteigas. With stunning landscapes, Manteigas is a municipality with 4 parishes, in the district of Guarda in the Centre region of Portugal. Manteigas shares with the municipalities of Seia and Covilhã, the highest point in mainland Portugal at 1993 meters of altitude. The main village of Manteigas is located in the Zêzere Glacier Valley, which is a great example of landscape modeling by glaciers, with its &apos;U&apos; shape. Historically, the most important economic activities in Manteigas are related to pastoralism, sheep&apos;s cheese production and wool industry.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 123.28. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 496. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 1993. The total population (in thousand) is 3.007. Full narrative:
## Suckling goat from Malaga
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is SPAIN ADEGUA. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 31 ES. There are about 300,000 head of Malaga goats in Spain. About 200,000 in the province of Malaga, distributed in more than 1,500 goat farms, the largest European concentration of this livestock. The suckling goat is an animal of one month of age, with a live weight between 8 and 10 kilos, fed exclusively on mother&apos;s milk. The breed is controlled through a Stud Book or certification by the Spanish Association of Malaga Goat Breeders (Cabrama). The &apos;Marca de Garantía Chivo Lechal Malagueño&apos; (Malaga Suckling Goat Guarantee Label) implies the fulfillment of an exhaustive set of conditions. Malaga suckling goat meat is increasingly consumed in the province of Malaga and beyond. 80% of the cattle farms have some territorial base so that the predominant management in the farms is semi-extensive. It confers unique properties, aroma, and flavors to the meat and milk, transmitted to the cheeses and the rest of the derived products.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Betic Systems. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Canillas de Aceituno. The Sierras Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama, are Natural Park since 1999. With its 2,065 m. altitude and very diverse flora, its lands are suitable for the cultivation of vineyards, olive groves, and fruit trees such as orange, fig, and loquat, etc. Its marginal character has favoured the conservation of the landscape, traditional architecture, and its natural values.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 42. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 150. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 2050. The total population (in thousand) is 1.68. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 40. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been -26.50%. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 96.63. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been 1.12%.

## Income and gross value added
The average per capita income (EUR/year) [Province] is 8587.56. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Province] is 28075.1. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 2.88%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 14.62%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 82.50%. The per capita income (EUR/year) [Region] is 16409.45. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Region] is 150440. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 6.53%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 20.01%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 73.46%.

## Tourism
The total number of bed places (BPs) in tourist accommodations/year in the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 573. The regional (Region) share of total BPs/year is 0,10%. The number of agricultural holdings in the LAU is 226. The nearest settlement with population over 100000 (km) is 47.6. In this region protected areas are present.

## Employment
The primary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 4.21%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 12.62%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 83.17%. The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 9.44%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 16.41%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 74.15%.

## Interest on this VC
The Malaga goat, a native Spanish breed, is highly prized for its milk production and its suckling goat meat quality. It has excellent fertility rates, being the Malaga milk goat, the first Spanish goat meat, and the first fresh meat in Andalusia, to have a quality label. The Malaga goat sector has a great potential for job creation. In addition to agri-food, with all its ancillary industry, catering and gastronomy or agrotourism, there are still other opportunities to be exploited. Cosmetics, linked to goat&apos;s milk, or all the goat skin crafts are two examples of lines to be developed.

## Key local assets
Malaga goat farming performs important environmental tasks through local resources, fire prevention, soil and biodiversity maintenance, and ecosystem conservation. The Malaga goat generates landscape and takes advantage of crop residues and marginal areas that other livestock types cannot graze. The management of the Natural Park promotes the integration of the goatherds with the natural space management.

## Challenges
The greatest challenge is keeping the farms alive in the face of generational replacement problems, excessive bureaucracy, lack of nearby slaughterhouses, low meat prices that remain the same as 20 years ago, and the need to establish an articulation of the sector. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Endogenous and Exogenous. Much has been done to promote the quality and differentiated value of this meat. Unfortunately, a strong business model has not yet been secured to accompany the development of new strategies, in any case, associated with some local restaurants that have innovated using the product, its processing, and online sales to take home processed product. The innovation is related to New processes, New marketing strategies and New governance systems. The land use systems involved are Grassland. The local assets include Natural, social and cultural.

## Concluding remarks
The total census of Malaga goats is approximately 300,000 head, including the province of Malaga, with about 200,000 specimens. It is found in other Andalusian areas, Spanish regions, Portugal, and northern Morocco.

## FIREWOOD FOR HEATING and COOKING
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is ROMANIA. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 RO. In the reference landscape chosen the key actors in the certified value chain are private forest owners (in other regions there is more state- and/or church-owned forest), professional woodcutters and firewood traders. The professional woodcutters have the expertise to select the most appropriate trees to harvest for firewood, plus the experience of working on the steep mountain slopes characteristic of the area. The firewood traders have storage space, transportation and a reliable supply of available labour for the intensive work of cutting and splitting the firewood.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Southern Romanian Carpathian Mountains. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Moeciu commune in Braşov County (RO122). Braşov county is a hilly mountainous region (500 - 2000 metres altitude) located in the south-eastern part of Transylvania. The relief is a diverse mix of mountainous (35%), hilly (50%) and lower altitude plain areas (15%) with an associated land use of forests (mainly in the mountain areas), permanent grasslands (mainly in the hilly areas), cultivated arable fields (in the hilly areas and lower altitude plains and hill areas) plus some urban, industrial and water surfaces (lakes and reservoirs).

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 110. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is unspecified. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is unspecified. The total population (in thousand) is 4.722. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 42.93. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) is unspecified. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 68.2. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been -0.90%.

## Income and gross value added
The average per capita income (EUR/year) [Province] is 12600. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Province] is 6259.84. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 2.40%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 35.80%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 61.80%. The per capita income (EUR/year) [Region] is 10000. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Region] is 21007.63. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 4.40%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 38.20%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 57.40%.

## Tourism
The total number of bed places (BPs) in tourist accommodations/year in the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 17795. The regional (Region) share of total BPs/year is 39.20%. The number of agricultural holdings in the LAU is 47980. The nearest settlement with population over 100000 (km) is unspecified. In this region protected areas are present.

## Employment
The primary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 8.80%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 40.30%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 51.20%. The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 9.90%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 42.20%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 48.00%.

## Interest on this VC
Wood continues to be very important fuel for heating and cooking in many households in mountain communities in Romania, including in Moeciu commune in the western extreme of Braşov County where households are very dispersed and basic services such as a public gas supply are poorly developed. This dependence upon firewood puts the local forest resources under great pressure - notably from unregulated and often illegal harvesting and collection of firewood for personal use and/or sale.

## Key local assets
Predominantly mixed decidious forest, especially areas which are most accessible for motorised or horse-drawn vehicles.

## Challenges
The harvesting / collection of firewood (mainly beech and birch with some oak, ash, spruce and fir) has been much more strictly regulated in the last few years and commercial sales must be accompanied by a certificate of origin. The records of registered firewood traders are regularly inspected and spot-checks by the police and environmental inspectors (&apos;Garda de Mediu&apos;) are also common. However, the increased regulation of firewood (and reduced supply) has contributed to a significant increase in price - around 250% in 10 years - which now provides additional incentive for the illegal trade in firewood to continue and flourish. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current. This VC is classified as Traditional.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is unspecified. The innovation is related to the whole VC. The land use systems involved are Forestry. The local assets include Natural.

## Different varieties of potatos produce under specific rules
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is ITALY UNIMOL and UNIPI. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 31 IT. The enhancement of the Montese potato was carried out through the redefinition of the organization of the production chain and the definition of a targeted promotion, as well as through an effective process of improving the quality and sustainability of the production process, the traceability and identifiability of the mountain product.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Northern Apennines - Tuscan-Emilian Apennines. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Montese. Montese is a municipality in the province of Modena, Emilia-Romagna region - Italy. It is located about 55 Km South of chief city Modena and 60 km southwest of Bologna. Montese has many woods over its territory, particularly centuries-old chestnut woods, it is also rich in terms of forage areas and for the typical Montese potatoes production.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 81.01. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 254. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 1126. The total population (in thousand) is 3.275. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 40.42. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been -2.55%. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 198.61. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been 3.44%.

## Income and gross value added
The average per capita income (EUR/year) [Province] is 39100. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Province] is 24664.4. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 1.69%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 40.23%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 58.07%. The per capita income (EUR/year) [Region] is 36200. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Region] is 144710. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 2.51%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 31.47%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 66.02%.

## Tourism
The total number of bed places (BPs) in tourist accommodations/year in the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 523. The regional (Region) share of total BPs/year is 0.11%. The number of agricultural holdings in the LAU is 305. The nearest settlement with population over 100000 (km) is 60. In this region protected areas are not present.

## Employment
The primary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 2.58%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 34.75%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 62.67%. The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 3.06%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 28.03%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 68.91%.

## Interest on this VC
Emilia Romagna is the region with the hight production of potatos in Italy, following by Campania and Tuscany. The main production is done in flat and irrigated land but some producer have start to introduce some innovation also in mountain area help by specific PEI measure of Rural Development Programme (Regional) - Emilia Romagna. For many years potatos were considered as a commodity product with a basic price. Only the last years, to mitigate the reduction of per capita consuption, were introduce new marketing strategies based on the traciability of the product thanks to the valorisation of local productions. By improving the profitability conditions of mountain agri-food production it is possible to generate positive effects on the mountain territory by reducing its depopulation.

## Key local assets
The research project, done under the RDP 4 , intends to provide the operators of the &apos;Montese Potato&apos; value chain with some useful tools to improve the perceived value of their mountain product. In fact, with the adoption of the European brand &apos;Mountain Product&apos; in conjunction with the &apos;Patata di Montese&apos; brand, producers can improve the brand awareness of their customers and therefore the perceived value of their products. Furthermore, referring to the product packaging and distribution phase, the project aims to identify the packaging system and coordinate the collaboration with the distribution to increase the penetration of distribution in the points of sale and support producers in the development of appropriate marketing activities.

## Challenges
The farm located in mountain area as: Azienda Agricola Terrasanta, Azienda Agricola Veranatura, Azienda Agricola Belvedere and Azienda Agricola Farini d&apos;Olmo have start to introduce new marketing strategies , using the label &apos;Mountain Product&apos; (Reg.(UE) n.1151/2012 and Reg.(UE) n.665/2014). Other as Azienda Agricola Palazzino have decide to use a specific label done by the local Chamber of Commerce &apos;Patata di montese&apos; to valorise the product. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Endogenous. The enhancement of the Montese potato was carried out through the redefinition of the organization of the production chain and the definition of a targeted promotion, as well as through an effective process of improving the quality and sustainability of the production process, the traceability and identification of the mountain products. The innovation is related to New marketing strategies. The land use systems involved are Cropland. The local assets include Social.

[Mountain landscape and reference chains] The reference mountain chain of this VC is Scandinavean Mountains - Scandes. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Oppdal. Most of the surface of Oppdal is mountainous area, and much of it is above the tree line. There are various lakes and several streams and rivers surrounded by prunes and pines. Nontheless, the main tree specie is the birch. Above the treeline, we can find heathers and alpine meadows, which provide grazing for cattles during summer. In fact, Oppdal region is considered to have exceptionally good grazing land by the Norwegian Institute of Forest and Nature. The average temperatures go from -5 in winter to 11 in summer, with possible snow precipitations until May.

Full narrative:
## Meat products (dry and cured meat)
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is SKANDINAVIAN UCO. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 SCA. The meat comes mostly and preferably from the same region. The company produces the cured meat in a farm in the mountains, using the dry wind from the mountains for the process. The products are sold in the region and all along Norway. This VC is part of the regional brand &apos;Oppdal- taste of mountains&apos; that guarantees the local origin of the product.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Scandinavean Mountains - Scandes. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Oppdal. Most of the surface of Oppdal is mountainous area, and much of it is above the tree line. There are various lakes and several streams and rivers surrounded by prunes and pines. Nontheless, the main tree specie is the birch. Above the treeline, we can find heathers and alpine meadows, which provide grazing for cattles during summer. In fact, Oppdal region is considered to have exceptionally good grazing land by the Norwegian Institute of Forest and Nature. The average temperatures go from -5 in winter to 11 in summer, with possible snow precipitations until May.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 2274.12. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 240. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 1985. The total population (in thousand) is 7.001. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 4.28. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been 6.03%. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 11.11. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been 10.08%.

## Income and gross value added
The average per capita income (EUR/year) [Province] is 58578.69. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Province] is 16471.96. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 2.59%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 19.25%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 78.16%. The per capita income (EUR/year) [Region] is 54460.72. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Region] is 22400.21. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 5.35%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 16.31%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 78.34%.

## Tourism
The total number of bed places (BPs) in tourist accommodations/year in the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 1626. The regional (Region) share of total BPs/year is 10.15%. The number of agricultural holdings in the LAU is 2 789. The nearest settlement with population over 100000 (km) is 118. In this region protected areas are present.

## Employment
The primary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 2.37%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 27.02%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 70.61%. The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 2.54%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 20.19%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 77.27%.

## Interest on this VC
High quality product, which is part of the traditional Norwegian food culture. The VC continues in the region because the employees of the former cured meat company have continued with the business, adding new products and new marketing strategies. Mostly related to e-commerce.

## Key local assets
The natural assets are the animals and also the mountain wind to cure the meat. The social asset relates to the regional brand as a way of cooperation among the producers. Finally, the cured meat is a traditional meal in the country and represents part of the food heritage.

## Challenges
1) Market competition with industrial meat corporations; 2) Abandonment of farms in the region that can affect the local supply of meat for transformation (in the municipality of Oppdal 40% of the farms stoped their activity between 1999 and 2014). Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current and Predicted. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Exogenous. The innovation relates to e-commerce marketing and the participation in regional brands. The case here described is part of Oppdal Smak av Fjeld but in Norway these kind of regional labels and producers&apos; associations seem quite common. The innovation is related to New marketing strategies. The land use systems involved are Cropland, Grassland and Mountain wind. The local assets include Natural, social and cultural.

Full narrative:
## Lamb Meat from Zuheros
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is SPAIN ADEGUA. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 32 ES. In the municipality of Zuheros there are about nine livestock farms that usually combine sheep and goats, most of them in semi-extensive regime, totaling about 10,000 head of livestock. Each sheep farm has an average of 500 sheep grazing on several hundred hectares of mountain range. The main resource is obtained from the sale of sheep with an average weight of ten kilos and a price close to 50 € / piece, stable for years. The sheep are sold to feedlots located mainly in the provinces of Granada and Murcia. There is no sectoral associative structure and certified organic production is not very relevant.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Betic System. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Zuheros. Sheep farming with similar characteristics extends to other municipalities of the Sierras Subbéticas Cordobesas (Cabra, Priego de Córdoba, Luque and Carcabuey), and also to areas of the province of Córdoba and other Andalusian provinces such as Granada and Seville. Greece, Romania, Italy and France are other EU countries where sheep farming is important.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 42.36. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 500. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 1300. The total population (in thousand) is 0.635. The population density (Inhabitants/km2) in the LAU is 14.99. The population change rate of the LAU in the last 10 years (%) has been -17.20%. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 97. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been -0.01%.

## Income and gross value added
The average per capita income (EUR/year) [Province] is 9426.19. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Province] is 13153.1. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 10.50%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 17.61%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Province] is 71.89%. The per capita income (EUR/year) [Region] is 16409.45. The total Gross Value Added (GVA) (EUR million/year) [Region] is 150440. The primary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 6.53%. The secondary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 20.01%. The tertiary sector share of total GVA/year [Region] is 73.46%.

## Tourism
The total number of bed places (BPs) in tourist accommodations/year in the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 169. The regional (Region) share of total BPs/year is 0,03%. The number of agricultural holdings in the LAU is 151. The nearest settlement with population over 100000 (km) is 65.9. In this region protected areas are present.

## Employment
The primary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 12.90%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 20.62%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Province] is 66.48%. The primary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 9.44%. The secondary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 16.41%. The tertiary sector share of total employment/year [Region] is 74.15%.

## Interest on this VC
Traditionally, the Andalusian mountains have hosted an extensive livestock activity, mainly goats and sheep, based on the use of pastures and focused on the production of goat&apos;s milk and cheese, as well as the sale of goats and lambs for meat.

## Key local assets
The resources in terms of farms, pastures, livestock and shepherds are local, although the infrastructure and housing associated with these farms have sometimes been abandoned or are in serious disrepair. There are no known plans for sustainable forest and pastures management. Supplementary feed is purchased from outside the production area.

## Challenges
It is a value chain with very little innovation that must ensure sustainable management of the forest and pastures, gain in diversification and fight against the difficult generational renewal of shepherds and average profitability of farms. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current. This VC is classified as Traditional.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is unspecified. There are no innovative elements in the value chain, which represents a serious challenge to guarantee the medium-term sustainability of these farms, which are also sometimes located in areas that are difficult to access and do not have facilities that make management attractive. The innovation is related to the whole VC. The land use systems involved are Grassland. The local assets include Natural, social and cultural.

## Concluding remarks
Sheep farming with similar characteristics extends to other municipalities of the Sierras Subbéticas Cordobesas (Cabra, Priego de Córdoba, Luque and Carcabuey), and also to areas of the province of Córdoba and other Andalusian provinces such as Granada and Seville. Greece, Romania, Italy and France are other EU countries where sheep farming is important.

## Potatoes grown in high altitudes, known by their quality
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is SERBIA. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 SER. Potatoes are grown mostly by small famer households. The land plots are small (reparceling was not implemented), growing is extensive, therefore yields are small (up to 10t/ha). There are several bigger companies that are subcontracting farmers for production, but many individual households also produce potatoes and sell them on the green markets or through the direct sale. Companies sell potatoes at the retailer stores, and/or export them to the region.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Western Serbia, Dinaric Alps region. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Ivanjica. Ivanjica is mountainous municipality with the highest pick at 1833m. The area is part of the Mountain Golija (Dinaric mountains) which is protected Natural Park. Almost 50% of the area is under the forests, from deciduous forest to conifer forests. The area is rich in rivers and water. Rivers are mountainous rivers with, habitat of many different river fish / interesting for sport fishing. Many wild animals live in the area of Ivanjica/Golija mountain forests, some of them endangered. The area is bordering with Bosnia and Herzegovina. Due to its specific climate, it is well known as European climate spa - favourable for specific rehabilitation. Besides potatoes, the area is known by raspberries and livestock husbandry. Beekeeping and NTFP are also present in the area. Ivanjica is much known for its cultural assets, as well as on comedy festival, existing for more than 50 years. There are many natural monuments and churches in the area.

## Geography and population
The size of the area (km2) is 1090. The minimum altimetry of the LAU (Local Administrative Unit) is 468. The maximum altimetry of the LAU is 1833. The total population (in thousand) is 29.49. The overall regional population density (Inhabitants/km2) is 72. The regional population change rate in the last 10 years (%) has been -6.53%.

## Interest on this VC
Ivanjica potatoes (Ivanjiski krompir) are PDO registered product at Serbian level (Protected Designation of Origin). This potato has a long tradition of production and very high reputation in Serbia, as well as in the region. The territory is spread over 8 municipalities (LAU1 level) including two NUTS3 regions, but the central municipality of production is Ivanjica (LAU1), while surrounding municipalities have just parts of their territory under this production (bordering with Ivanjica municipality). It has a very long tradition of growing in this area / since the end of 19th century, when one of the major products for market were potatoes. Family farming is prevailing. According to the last Agricultural census conducted in 2012 in Serbia, 4.356,67 ha, this makes around 17% of the total prodcution of the potatoes in Serbia. Municipality of Ivanjica is part of Golija Mountain, which is protected as Natural Park (IUCN Ib protection), with its highest peak at 1.833m. Additionally at the territory of Ivanjica there is also protected Landscape of outstanding feature Mali Rzav (spring of river Rzav). Raspberry growing and forestry are also very important VCs in the area. Ivanjica is also known by the prefabricated houses, built from the good quality wood from the area. Climate of the region is humid continental with long and strong winter and hot and short summer. Ivanjica potatoes are very important for the area, as they are mainly produced by family farming households. Current situation is not favourable for the production, and it affect the quantities produced for a longer period. Import of potatoes from other countries brings the necessity to differentiate this product from the imported ones. This can be done by new marketing strategies, labelling, initiating organic production, etc.

## Key local assets
Due to specific climate conditions, Ivanjica is exclusive European climate spa, with Institute for specific rehabilitation. The area is rich in mountainous rivers full with fish, making the area interesting for sport fishing. Landscapes and scenery are beautiful, attracting tourists, and that should be preserved. The area is full with minerals and possibilities for mining (antimony, craft stones, specific stones used for roofs, etc.). Almost 50% of the area is under forests, rich in non-forest products and wild animals, some of them are endangered species.

## Challenges
Prices of potatoes were very low in 2020, causing big quantities of potatoes in stock at the end of the season. Producers are facing problems due to the bad year, with additional burden of this remaining stocks. Areas under potatoes are reducing, and it can be expected that this trend will continue. Overall the type of challenge faced by this VC is Current and Predicted. This VC is classified as Traditional and Innovative.

## Innovation
The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Exogenous. Certification of the Ivanjica potatoes as protected designation origin, and marekting under this label should bring added value and differentiation on the markets, both on national and international (especially if the product would be protected at EU level at the later stage). The innovation is related to New marketing strategies and New governance systems. The land use systems involved are Cropland, Grassland, Forestry and Natural land. The local assets include Natural, social, cultural and Natural park and protected landscape of outstanding feature.

## Concluding remarks
Potatoes are produced in oder mountain regions too, but Ivanjica potatoes have the highest reputation among customers, due to its high quality and taste.

## Different varieties of hazel nuts ( Mortarella, San giovanni, Camponica, Giffoni)
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is ITALY UNIMOL and UNIPI. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 32 IT. The mountain productions need to close the supply chain to be more competive and to give added value to an excellent product.

## Sheep&apos;s wool processing helps maintain traditional sheep farming in Slovakia; the sheep wool products include home textile products and clothes
The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is SLOVAKIA. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 04 SK. Key activities: sheep breeding,wool selling, wool processing, marketing. Key actors: sheep breeders, sheep wool processors, consumers. Positive impact on local and regional SES through retro innovation of wool processing and products. Moreover, it helps to use the otherwise waste product.

## Mountain landscape and reference chains
The reference mountain chain of this VC is Slovak Carpathians. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Banská Štiavnica. Banská Štiavnica is a completely preserved medieval town. Because of their historical value, the town and its surroundings were proclaimed by the UNESCO to be a World Heritage Site on December 11, 1993. In history, it was an important mining place. The town is surounded by landscape with high aethetic value.
